scope: Coheronia v0.1 MVP vertical slice one-shot implementation (Godot 4.6), constrained to docs/MVP_VERTICAL_SLICE.md.
summary: Playable settlement-loop prototype: mine/place blocks with hardness timing, torches with dynamic visible light, Town Hall stockpile/deposit/repair, Coherence/Load/Resilience computed from real world state using formulas evaluated from data/settlement_rules.json, day/night cycle with a night slime threat event, and F5/F9 save/load. Verified by a 22-check automated smoke test through real gameplay code paths plus reviewed screenshot.
acceptance: Godot project imports clean with no missing script/resource references (Godot 4.6.1 headless --import)
Main scene launches; 11,868 terrain cells generated from seed
Player moves and jumps (smoke: dx=34, jump velocity -300)
Mining works for dirt/wood/stone with hardness ordering (21/33/66 frames) and inventory drops
Block placement consumes inventory; torch placement creates a live PointLight2D
Town Hall exists, core blocks protected from mining, deposit raises stockpile
C/L/R react to world state: Coherence 31.2->53.2 when torches placed near hall; Load 12.3->32.3 under forced night threat
Threat entities spawn at nightfall and damage the hall on contact
F5/F9 save/load round trip restores terrain deltas, placed blocks, torch lights, player position, inventory, and stockpile
HUD renders health, hotbar, C/L/R bars, status labels, day/night, event log (screenshot reviewed)
validation: capsule_doctor.py --profile private_repo -> usable (git warning resolved by initializing repo)
COHERONIA_SMOKE=1 headless run -> SMOKE RESULT: PASS (22/22), exit 0
decisions: TileSet (colored tiles + collision polygons) generated at runtime; no imported art assets, placeholder visuals via _draw()
C/L/R formulas evaluated from data/settlement_rules.json strings via Godot Expression class to keep data files authoritative
Saves persist world_seed + terrain deltas only; base terrain regenerates deterministically
HUD built in code on a thin CanvasLayer scene to minimize hand-written .tscn risk
Git repo initialized this run per manifest git_policy.commit_after_signable_run; not pushed (push_policy manual)
risks: No human playthrough yet; tuning unverified by a person
Active threats not persisted in saves (despawn on load; night pressure re-derives from time)
blocks_light per-tile occlusion present in data but not simulated in v0.1
next_action: Operator playthrough in Godot 4.6.1 for feel/tuning, then scope v0.2.
